Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for the Mercer loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with a query like "Mercer" or "Evan Mercer." This returns loan LN-2026-00184, conventional, $340,000 loan amount, rate 6.875%, status "processing", assigned LO Carlos Rivera, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-23, with notes referencing new construction and build delays causing rate lock extensions.
OE 2: Search Stripe charges for Mercer lock extensions using stripe_list_charges or stripe_search_charges filtered by the Mercer customer (cus_scenario_c66_mercer) or loan number. This returns three lock extension charges: ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 for $375 ("Rate lock extension -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)"), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 for $375 ("Rate lock extension (2nd)"), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 for $500 ("Rate lock extension (3rd)"). Total collected via Stripe: $1,250.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks for Mercer lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills and the rate lock extension expense account using quickbooks_search_accounts. Three QB bills are found with issues: BILL-2026-0319-001 ($375, DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, blank line detail, unpaid placeholder), BILL-2026-0402-001 ($375, DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1, "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay", paid), and BILL-2026-0416-002 ($375, DocNumber PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER, "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership", unpaid). No bill exists for the 3rd lock extension charge ($500). Account 6351 "Rate Lock Extension Fees" has a current balance of $375.
OE 4: Create a new bill in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_bill for the missing 3rd lock extension charge (~$500) referencing the Mercer loan (LN-2026-00184), vendor V-KESTONE-OPS or similar, and account 6351 (Rate Lock Extension Fees).
OE 5: Add an activity note on loan LN-2026-00184 in the mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_add_activity, documenting the billing reconciliation findings (3 Stripe charges totaling $1,250, QB gaps identified, missing 3rd extension bill posted).
OE 6: Look up Marcus Webb in the LOS staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff or mortgage_los_get_staff. This returns Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a), role loan_officer, is_active: true, termination_date: null. The LOS has not been updated to reflect his departure.
OE 7: List connected Stripe accounts using stripe_list_connected_accounts and identify Marcus Webb's account. This returns acct_scenario_644_departed with business_name "Marcus Webb Consulting", type express, business_type individual, email marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com, charges_enabled true, payouts_enabled true. Metadata reveals staff_id los_staff_a583f044387a and departed "2026-03-15". The account is still active with payouts enabled despite his March 15, 2026 departure.
OE 8: List Stripe transfers filtered by destination account acct_scenario_644_departed using stripe_list_transfers. Five post-departure commission transfers are found: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn $1,250 (LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 $1,875 (LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 $980 (LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs $1,450 (LN-2026-00211, auto_generated), and tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA $2,100 (LN-2026-00608, auto_generated). Total post-departure exposure: $7,655.
OE 9: Search emails and Slack messages for "Marcus Webb" or related terms using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. This surfaces emails showing Webb as an active LO on past loans, Slack messages flagging a potential conflict of interest with Danielle Webb as buyer's agent, and additional context about his departure and pipeline reassignment.
OE 10: Send an email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com using send_email with a financial summary of the Marcus Webb post-departure commission exposure: 5 transfers totaling approximately $7,655, loan-by-loan breakdown (LN-2026-00525/$1,250, LN-2026-00527/$1,875, LN-2026-00008/$980, LN-2026-00211/$1,450, LN-2026-00608/$2,100), Webb's Stripe Connect account still active with payouts enabled, departure date March 15, 2026.
OE 11: Send a separate email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com using send_email focused on the compliance angle: a departed employee's Stripe Connect payout channel (acct_scenario_644_departed) was never deactivated after his March 15 departure, commission transfers continued flowing, payouts_enabled still true, total funds paid post-departure approximately $7,655.
OE 12: Post a message in the #compliance-alerts Slack channel (C004) using conversations_add_message alerting about Marcus Webb's post-departure commission transfers, Stripe Connect account still active, and total exposure of approximately $7,655.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks invoices for Russell Ibrahim using quickbooks_search_invoices. This returns invoice INV-c569e6bf9158 (DocNumber KMP-00192), TxnDate 2024-12-18, TotalAmt $5,434.79. The line item shows description "Broker compensation -- Russell Ibrahim (LN-2024-00192)", Amount $5,434.79, but UnitPrice $5,584.79 (Qty 1). There is a $150 discrepancy between the invoiced total and the line item unit price.
OE 14: Cross-reference the Ibrahim loan in the mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan with "Ibrahim" or "LN-2024-00192." This returns loan LN-2024-00192, FHA, loan_amount $760,000, purchase_price $800,000, status "closed", closing_date 2024-12-18, assigned LO Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a). The loan data provides reference for verifying the broker compensation calculation.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks for Premier Data Solutions bills and vendor record using quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_vendors. This returns vendor record Id 143 ("Premier Data Solutions") with billing address 4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209 (a UPS Store/mailbox address, not a commercial office). Bill B-2108 found: $600, "Monthly data services", DocNumber PDS-030126. No contract, PO, or engagement letter on file.
OE 16: Search emails and Slack for Premier Data Solutions context using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. This surfaces: an email from Hana to Grace stating she has no formal contract packet and assumed Premier Data Solutions was related to an IT service Raj had set up; a Slack message from Victor in C001 reporting 8 straight months of $600 invoices all approved by Hana with no vendor setup docs or contract found; and CRM engagements documenting a multi-step investigation where Grace called Hana (no contract produced), Raj denied the vendor, the billing address was identified as a UPS Store, ghost-vendor pattern was suspected, and an audit review was scheduled.
OE 17: Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement documenting the Premier Data Solutions investigation findings: vendor billing address (4216 Park Rd PMB 118, UPS Store), no contract or PO on file, at least $600 in documented QB bills for "Monthly data services", Hana's email citing a Raj connection with no formal agreement, Victor's Slack flag about recurring invoices, and the existing CRM investigation trail showing ghost-vendor pattern concern.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The Stripe account metadata and LOS staff data don't show a specific departure date; the agent inferred late March from Slack context. The criterion requires approximately March 15 or mid-March, which this email does not satisfy.
✗Run #4The email to Grace states Marcus Webb's notice date as March 22, 2026 and last day as April 4, 2026. The criterion requires approximately March 15, 2026 (from Stripe Connect metadata departed: 2026-03-15). Neither March 22 nor April 4 qualifies as approximately March 15.
✗Run #5The Grace email states Marcus submitted 'his two-week notice on approximately March 22, 2026 with a last day of April 4, 2026.' The criterion requires approximately March 15, 2026 as his departure date (from the Stripe Connect account metadata). The agent reported March 22 (notice) / April 4 (last day), not March 15.
✓Run #6The Grace email states 'Marcus Webb resigned on or about March 22, 2026.' The criterion accepts 'March 2026' as sufficient. While the specific date differs from March 15, the email provides a March 2026 date reference satisfying this criterion.

26 The final response identifies Marcus Webb's departure date as approximately March 15, 2026 (or mid-March 2026).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✓ 17%
✗Run #1The agent reports Marcus Webb's departure as 'last day of April 4, 2026' (with a notice submitted March 22), not approximately March 15, 2026. The Stripe Connect account metadata shows departed: 2026-03-15, but the agent either did not find this metadata or used a different data source. April 4, 2026 does not satisfy 'approximately March 15, 2026 or mid-March 2026'.
✗Run #2The final response states departure 'was confirmed by an email from borrower Stephen Hamilton (~March 27, 2026)' — this is the date of the email, not Marcus Webb's actual departure date. The response does not identify approximately March 15, 2026 as the departure date. The Stripe Connect account metadata returned no explicit 'departed' field with a March 15 date.
✗Run #3The final response states Marcus Webb departed 'around March 25-27, 2026,' not approximately March 15, 2026. Neither the Stripe Connect account metadata nor the LOS staff data in the trajectory showed a specific March 15 departure date, and Slack context pointed to late March. The criterion requires approximately March 15 or mid-March.
✗Run #4The agent reports Marcus Webb's notice date as March 22, 2026 and last day as April 4, 2026. The criterion requires approximately March 15, 2026 (from Stripe Connect metadata departed: 2026-03-15). The agent did not surface March 15 as the departure date anywhere in the final response.
✗Run #5The final response states Marcus 'submitted his two-week notice on ~March 22, 2026, with a last day of April 4, 2026.' The criterion requires the departure date to be approximately March 15, 2026 (from Stripe Connect account metadata departed: 2026-03-15). The agent reported March 22/April 4, not March 15.
✓Run #6The final response states 'Marcus resigned ~March 22, 2026'. The criterion accepts 'March 2026' as a valid form of the departure date. While the agent identified March 22 from Slack (vs. March 15 from Stripe metadata), the response provides a specific March 2026 date reference.
